title: thelypteris repandula
intro:
Thelypteris repandula, also known as the Japanese holly fern, is a species of fern belonging to the family Thelypteridaceae, characterized by its distinctive holly-like fronds and native to East Asia.

This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, such as diarrhea and constipation, due to its ability to regulate bowel movements and soothe the digestive system. The health benefits of Thelypteris repandula include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce swelling and prevent infection in wounds. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and teas, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies such as poultices and salves.
